tipRules.tip: KVD: 'Fall with 2ft visibility — Red Eye Shad 1/2oz is my absolute favorite. Vary line size to control depth: 17lb fluoro for 2ft zone, lighter for deeper. Must contact bottom or nip grass. Pumping retrieve — pull/stop/pull like a jig only faster. The unique fluttering fall triggers bites. Sexy Shad color; perch/bluegill in northern lakes.'

KVD: 'Fall dirty water after heavy rain — head farther back in creeks into the dirty water. Incoming stained water pulls shad toward current and bass follow. Fish get extremely shallow and tight to cover. That's when a squarebill shines.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Fall hotspot indicators: gulls, cormorants, and herons point to baitfish concentrations. Watch for coots — they sit and feed over grass beds, preferring the greener, healthier grass that also attracts bait and bass.'
